key_sentences.chinese: 既然你已经知道她喜欢什么，就用心去为她准备吧！
key_sentences.pinyin: Jì rán nǐ yǐjīng zhīdào tā xǐhuān shénme, jiù yòng xīn qù wèi tā zhǔn bèi ba.
key_sentences.english: Since you already know what she likes, then just prepare for her thoughtfully.

grammar_points.title: 居然 — Surprisingly / Unexpectedly
grammar_points.explanation: 居然 is an adverb placed before the verb to express that something happened contrary to the speaker's expectation — often with a sense of surprise or disbelief. Structure: Subject + 居然 + verb phrase. It can be used for positive or negative surprises. 他居然哭了 - He actually cried — I didn't expect that. 这么难的题她居然做对了 - She surprisingly got such a difficult question right.

examples.chinese: 他居然记住了我所有朋友的名字。
examples.pinyin: Tā jū rán jì zhù le wǒ suǒyǒu péngyǒu de míngzi.
examples.english: He surprisingly remembered the names of all my friends.

examples.chinese: 他们分开了这么多年，居然还保持着联系。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen fēnkāi le zhème duō nián, jū rán hái bǎochí zhe liánxì.
examples.english: After so many years apart, they surprisingly still kept in touch.

exercises.question: 她平时很内向, 今天 ____ 主动来找我倾诉心情。
他们已经分手五年了，____ 还记得彼此的生日。
Which sentence uses 居然 correctly?
exercises.correct: 他居然一个人吃完了整个蛋糕。
options: 他居然一个人吃完了整个蛋糕。
他居然喜欢吃蛋糕，因为蛋糕很甜。
option_pinyin: tā jū rán yīgè rén chī wán le zhěnggè dàngāo.
tā jū rán xǐhuān chī dàngāo, yīnwèi dàngāo hěn tián.
jū rán tā míngtiān qù shàngbān.
tā chī dàngāo jū rán hěn tián.

mini_exercises.question: 居然 expresses...
mini_exercises.correct: Surprise or disbelief that an unexpected outcome occurred — placed before the verb

A logical result following a condition

tā wàng jì le wǒ men de jié hūn jì niàn rì jū rán

grammar_points.title: 如何 — How / In what way (formal)
grammar_points.explanation: 如何 is a formal question word equivalent to 怎么 (how) or 怎样 (in what way). It is more commonly used in written Chinese, formal speech, or serious/literary contexts. Structure: Subject + 如何 + verb / 如何 + verb phrase (at the start). Unlike 怎么, 如何 can also follow the topic as a predicate: 情况如何？(How is the situation?) It often appears in set phrases and essay writing. 我不知道如何是好 - I don't know what to do . 不管结果如何，我都会支持你 - No matter what the result is, I will support you.

examples.chinese: 我们应该思考如何更好地珍惜彼此。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yīnggāi sīkǎo rú hé gèng hǎo de zhēnxī bǐcǐ.
examples.english: We should think about how to better cherish each other.

examples.chinese: 不管生活如何变化，他们始终相互陪伴。
examples.pinyin: Bùguǎn shēnghuó rú hé biànhuà, tāmen shǐzhōng xiānghù péibàn.
examples.english: No matter how life changes, they have always kept each other company.

qǐng rú hé gào sù wǒ gǎi shàn fū qī zhī jiān de gōu tōng
rú hé qǐng gào sù wǒ gǎi shàn fū qī zhī jiān de gōu tōng
qǐng gào sù wǒ gǎi shàn fū qī zhī jiān de gōu tōng rú hé

grammar_points.title: 既然…就 — Since…then
grammar_points.explanation: 既然…就 is a cause-and-effect connective structure meaning 'since [a situation is already the case], then [conclusion or action follows].' Structure: 既然 + reason/given situation, Subject + 就 + conclusion/action. The 既然 clause presents an already-established fact or condition; the 就 clause draws a logical next step. This differs from 因为…所以 in that 既然 emphasizes an accepted premise, not a discovered reason. 既然你爱她，就应该告诉她 - Since you love her, you should tell her. 既然决定了，就不要后悔 - Since the decision has been made, don't regret it.

examples.chinese: 既然你已经感受到她的体贴，就应该好好珍惜她。
examples.pinyin: Jì rán nǐ yǐjīng gǎnshòu dào tā de tǐtiē, jiù yīnggāi hǎohǎo zhēnxī tā.
examples.english: Since you have already felt her thoughtfulness, you should truly cherish her.

examples.chinese: 既然这段关系让你温馨快乐，就不要轻易放弃。
examples.pinyin: Jì rán zhè duàn guānxi ràng nǐ wēnxīn kuàilè, jiù bú yào qīngyì fàngqì.
examples.english: Since this relationship makes you feel warm and happy, don't give it up easily.

exercises.question: ____ 你决定陪伴她，____ 要做到始终如一。
既然他已经向你表达了感情，你 ____ 给他一个明确的回答。
Which sentence correctly uses 既然…就?
exercises.correct: 既然你们有如此深厚的默契，就应该好好在一起。
options: 既然你们有如此深厚的默契，就应该好好在一起。
既然他很体贴，但她不喜欢他。
option_pinyin: jì rán nǐmen yǒu rúcǐ shēnhòu de mòqì, jiù yīnggāi hǎohǎo zài yīqǐ.
jì rán tā hěn tǐtiē, dàn tā bù xǐhuān tā.
jì rán wǒ jiù zhēnxī nǐ.
nǐ jì rán, jiù lái le ma?

exercises.question: 既然他们已经是多年的恩爱夫妻，____ 应该有很深的感情基础。
既然你无法用语言表达，____ 试试用行动来体贴她吧。

mini_exercises.question: 既然...就... expresses...
mini_exercises.correct: A logical deduction from an accepted fact: 'since X is the case, then Y naturally follows'
options: A logical deduction from an accepted fact: 'since X is the case, then Y naturally follows'
A hypothetical condition: 'if X were true, then Y'
A concession: 'even though X, Y still happens'

mini_exercises.question: Fill in: Since you have already decided, don't regret it.
mini_exercises.question_english: Since you have already decided, don't regret it.

mini_exercises.question: Which sentence correctly uses 既然…就?
mini_exercises.correct: 既然你喜欢她，就应该勇敢地表达。
options: 既然你喜欢她，就应该勇敢地表达。
你既然就喜欢她，应该勇敢地表达。
就既然你喜欢她，应该勇敢地表达。
你喜欢她既然，就应该勇敢地表达。
option_pinyin: jì rán nǐ xǐ huān tā jiù yīng gāi yǒng gǎn de biǎo dá
nǐ jì rán jiù xǐ huān tā yīng gāi yǒng gǎn de biǎo dá
jiù jì rán nǐ xǐ huān tā yīng gāi yǒng gǎn de biǎo dá
nǐ xǐ huān tā jì rán jiù yīng gāi yǒng gǎn de biǎo dá

culture_notes.title: The Origin of Chúxī — Chinese New Year's Eve
culture_notes.content:
Long ago, according to Chinese legend, a fearsome monster called 夕 (Xī) roamed the earth at the end of every year. Every New Year's Eve, it would descend from the mountains and terrorize villages — eating livestock, destroying crops, and frightening families. People were powerless against it and lived in dread of this one night each year.

One day, a wise elder discovered that the monster 夕 had three great fears: loud noises, bright fire, and the color red. Armed with this knowledge, people began hanging red lanterns and red paper couplets (对联) on their doors, setting off firecrackers (鞭炮), and lighting bonfires. They stayed up through the night to keep the fires burning — a practice that became known as 守岁 (shǒu suì), or 'keeping the year.' The monster was driven away, never to return.

This legend gave birth to the beloved traditions of Chinese New Year's Eve: red decorations, fireworks, and families staying up together until midnight. In the context of love, 除夕 holds a special tenderness. For Chinese families, gathering around the table on this night — sharing a meal, watching the Spring Festival Gala, listening to fireworks outside — is one of the most intimate and heartwarming 'details of love' in all of Chinese culture. To accompany (陪伴) someone through the turn of the year is considered one of the greatest expressions of affection.

idioms.english: bid farewell to the old and welcome the new

quiz.question: Which word means 'tacit understanding' or 'unspoken harmony'?
Fill in the blank: 她 ____ 记得我们第一次见面的日期，让我非常感动。
Which word is most appropriate? 这个房间布置得很____，让人感到非常舒适。
____你已经知道如何表达感情，就不要再犹豫了。
He is always considerate — he remembers every small detail. Which word best describes him?

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 你知道吗，他居然还记得我第一次提到喜欢玫瑰是在三年前。
lines.pinyin: Nǐ zhīdào ma, tā jū rán hái jì de wǒ dì yī cì tídào xǐhuān méiguī shì zài sān nián qián.
lines.english: You know, he surprisingly still remembered that the first time I mentioned liking roses was three years ago.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 哇，这就是爱的细节啊。这样的人真的很体贴。
lines.pinyin: Wā, zhè jiù shì ài de xì jié a. Zhèyàng de rén zhēn de hěn tǐtiē.
lines.english: Wow, that's exactly the detail of love. Someone like that is truly thoughtful.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 是啊。我一直在想，如何回应他的这种细心，才能让他也感受到我的心意。
lines.pinyin: Shì a. Wǒ yīzhí zài xiǎng, rú hé huíyìng tā de zhè zhǒng xìxīn, cái néng ràng tā yě gǎnshòu dào wǒ de xīnyì.
lines.english: Yeah. I've been thinking about how to respond to his attentiveness so that he can also feel my feelings.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 我觉得，最重要的是用心倾听他说话。这也是一种很深的陪伴。
lines.pinyin: Wǒ juéde, zuì zhòngyào de shì yòng xīn qīngtīng tā shuōhuà. Zhè yě shì yī zhǒng hěn shēn de péibàn.
lines.english: I think the most important thing is to listen wholeheartedly when he speaks. That is also a very deep form of companionship.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 说得对。其实偶尔不说话，就静静地陪在彼此身边，也是一种默契！
lines.pinyin: Shuō de duì. Qí shí ǒu ěr bù shuōhuà, jiù jìngjìng de péi zài bǐcǐ shēnbiān, yě shì yī zhǒng mòqì ba.
lines.english: That's true. Actually saying nothing occasionally and just quietly staying by each other's side — that's also a kind of unspoken harmony, isn't it.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 对，既然有这样恩爱的关系，就要好好珍惜，不是吗？
lines.pinyin: Duì, jì rán yǒu zhèyàng ēn ài de guānxi, jiù yào hǎohǎo zhēnxī, bú shì ma?
lines.english: Right — since you have such a loving relationship, you should truly cherish it, shouldn't you?

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 我最近养成了一个习惯，每晚睡前听同一个电台，感觉很温馨。
lines.pinyin: Wǒ zuìjìn yǎngchéng le yīgè xíguàn, měi wǎn shuì qián tīng tóngyīgè diàntái, gǎnjué hěn wēnxīn.
lines.english: I've recently developed a habit of listening to the same radio station before bed every night — it feels very warm and cozy.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 是一个人听，还是和她一起？
lines.pinyin: Shì yīgè rén tīng, háishì hé tā yīqǐ?
lines.english: Do you listen alone, or together with her?

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 一起听。我们都在家，一边做一些自己的事，一边听这个节目。这也算是一种陪伴吧。
lines.pinyin: Yīqǐ tīng. Wǒmen dōu zài jiā, yì biān zuò yì xiē zì jǐ de shì, yì biān tīng zhè gè jié mù. Zhè yě suàn shì yī zhǒng péibàn ba.
lines.english: Together. We're at home, doing our own things while listening to this program. That counts as a kind of keeping each other company.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 真好。特别是如果两人在两地分隔的时候，能有这样的温馨时刻很难得。
lines.pinyin: Zhèn hǎo. Tèbié shì rú guó liǎng rén zài liǎngdì fēngé de shíhou, néng yǒu zhèyàng de wēnxīn shíkè hěn nándé.
lines.english: What a lovely idea. Especially if two people are in different places, having such a heartwarming moment is truly precious.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 嗯，我们不在一起时，偶尔还会发信息说，"这首歌好听"，或者"主持人今天说得真有道理"。
lines.pinyin: Ń, wǒmen bú zài yì qǐ shí, ǒu ěr hái huì fā xìnxī shuō, 'zhè shǒu gē hǎotīng', huòzhě 'zhǔchírén jīntiān shuō de zhēn yǒu dàolǐ'.
lines.english: Yeah, when we're not together, we would occasionally send messages saying 'this song is beautiful' or 'the host said something really insightful today.'

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 这就是爱的细节。不需要多复杂，只要用心，就能让对方感受到你的陪伴。
lines.pinyin: Zhè jiù shì ài de xì jié. Bù xūyào duō fùzá, zhǐyào yòng xīn, jiù néng ràng duìfāng gǎnshòu dào nǐ de péibàn.
lines.english: That's the detail of love right there. It doesn't need to be complicated — as long as you are sincere, the other person will feel your companionship.
